text:
In this paper we investigate some techniques in order to alleviate the computational burden of the $\delta$-Generalized Labeled Multi-Bernoulli ($\delta$-GLMB) filter proposed in \cite{Shan_GGLMB_2017}, which is an analytical solution to the multi-target Bayes filter. This filter enables the tracking of an unknown and time-varying number of targets moving along differentiated trajectories. However, the update and prediction steps of the $\delta$-GLMB filter involves evaluating the weighted sum of multi-target exponentials, where the number of terms of the summation is almost exponentially increasing with the number of targets and their associated weights. In order to keep the filter efficient we investigate a set of truncation techniques in order to select the most relevant components of the weights of the multi-target exponential representation of the posterior probability distribution.

We apply ranked assignment algorithms in the measurement update and $K$-shortest path algorithms in the prediction step to efficiently extract the high weight paths in the graphs. To further improve the performance, we apply “look-ahead” techniques using faster methods such as the probability hypothesis density (PHD) filter for the truncation decision. In addition, we derive a characterization of the $L_1$-error caused by the truncation, and show that the introduced truncation method yields the minimal $L_1$-error between the exact and the approximate multi-target distribution. The performance of the proposed filter is verified by simulations and its robustness to anomalies associated with other random finite set filters is also illustrated.
